title: HOME YOUTH SERVICES - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 273-7300 Taylor's House Main phone (503) 391-6428 The Drop In Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: The Drop In: Walk in or call for information Taylor's House: Visit day center FEES: None DOCUMENTS: If 18 years old, proof of age and school enrollment AREA SERVED: Marion and Polk Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Second house from the corner of Church and Union Streets. Three-story house with lime green trim with "HOME" painted in the windows. Services: Community Meals * At Risk Youth Homeless Drop In Centers * At Risk Youth Public Internet Access Sites * Homeless Youth School Supplies Donation Programs Street Outreach Programs * At Risk Youth Youth Centers Youth Employment Programs Youth Shelters If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
